steps.title: Interpret the definition of tall
steps.content: A botanist classifies a plant as tall if $h > 100$ cm. For a compound inequality $a < h < b$, the smallest possible height is just above $a$. To ensure all heights satisfy $h > 100$, the lower bound $a$ must be greater than 100.

steps.title: Find the inequality with lower bound greater than 100
steps.content:
Checking the lower bound of each choice:
  • Choice A: $106 < h < 158$ has lower bound 106, and $106 > 100$ ✓
  • Choice B: $80 < h < 100$ has lower bound 80, and 80 is not greater than 100
  • Choice C: $42 < h < 87$ has lower bound 42, and 42 is not greater than 100
  • Choice D: $17 < h < 85$ has lower bound 17, and 17 is not greater than 100
Only choice A ensures all heights exceed 100. That matches choice A — A is correct.
